sevenDayForecast.headline: Severe Tropical Cyclone Errol is beginning to weaken and will start to track towards the west Kimberley coast today.
points: Tropical Cyclone Advices and Forecast Track Maps are being issued for Severe Tropical Cyclone Errol and should be referred to for more detailed information.
Severe Tropical Cyclone Errol has peaked at Category 4 intensity this morning, and is now showing signs of weakening.
Errol will start moving east southeast and towards the west Kimberley coast today.
There is a High chance it will remain at tropical cyclone strength until Friday night or early Saturday.

trackForecast.headline: Severe Tropical Cyclone Errol to start weakening and move towards the west Kimberley coast today.
trackForecast.discussion:
Severe Tropical Cyclone Errol (29U) recently had an eye on satellite imagery and was slow moving to the south so confidence in the location is high. It will start moving to the east southeast later this morning. 

The system has likely reached its peak intensity. Conditions will become unfavourable during today and, due to its small size, Errol is likely to weaken rapidly during Thursday and Friday. 

The extension of the 120 hour confidence cone towards offshore Pilbara waters is representing scenarios of a remnant weak shallow tropical low moving back towards the west over the weekend. A tropical cyclone is very unlikely to exist in this area.
trackForecast.discussionUpperBound: In the upper bound scenario, Errol maintains peak intensity for up to another six hours and weakens more slowly than forecast, avoiding significant influence from the upper trough. This scenario would also see a slower east-southeast movement, with the system potentially crossing the west Kimberley coast as late as Saturday night. As a small system, Errol remains prone to rapid fluctuations in intensity.
